title: What is the difference between the timeline and Gantt views?
excerpt: Trying to figure out the difference between the timeline and Gantt views, they seem very similar. Thanks for any insight!

posts.author: MonDude (MonDude)
posts.cookedHtml: Trying to figure out the difference between the timeline and Gantt views, they seem very similar. Thanks for any insight!

posts.author: haPartnerships (Matthew Longmate)
posts.cookedHtml:
Haven’t used them for a while but unless they have changed significantly the timeline view tends to find the best fit of pulses into as few rows as possible (multiple pulses on a single row) whereas the Gantt view (really not a Gantt chart by a far stretch of the imagination) tends to place a pulse per row much more like a traditional gantt chart (but lacks the things that make it a gantt chart such as tabular details next to the graphic or supporting predecessors and relationships etc).

So the bottom line is not much difference timeline multiple pulses per row, gantt single pulse per row.

posts.author: Sven.H (Sven Hausherr)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m also wondering. Whats the difference? Seams you have the exact same features in either one. Whats the difference really?
